channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: APC sets up committee to manage hotels and transport for 2026 convention
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don begin to prepare for their 2026 National Convention by inaugurating an Accommodation and Transportation Committee for proper planning. The committee wey dey Abu...

item.title: House Republicans Threaten Senate Blockade Over SAVE America Act
item.description: In this Fox News opinion piece, Representatives Randy Fine of Florida, Keith Self of Texas, and Mark Harris of North Carolina declare that House Republicans will block all Senate legislation until the...

item.title: NASA's Van Allen Probe A Expected to Re-Enter Earth's Atmosphere
item.description: NASA's Van Allen Probe A, a 1,300-pound (600 kg) spacecraft launched in 2012 to study Earth's radiation belts, is scheduled to re-enter Earth's atmosphere this week. The probe's descent is mostly expe...

item.title: Mindoro Officials Voice Opposition to Proposed Ferry Fare Increases Due to Surging Fuel Prices
item.description: Local officials in Mindoro have publicly opposed the recent announcements of shipping fare increases by several ferry operators serving routes to and from the island, particularly those connecting to ...

item.title: China raises concerns over US military use of AI and potential global risks
item.description: China has raised serious concerns about the United States’ growing reliance on artificial intelligence in its military operations. Officials warn that unrestricted AI use could lead to a dystopian fut...

item.title: Timaya share him honest thoughts on marriage and raising children without wedlock
item.description: Nigerian singer Inetimi Alfred Odon, wey most people sabi as Timaya, don cause plenty arguments online after video show am telling fellow musicians say e better to born pikin outside marriage than to ...

item.title: Tanzanians Stranded Abroad Return Safely, Call for Peace
item.description: On Monday evening, 236 Tanzanians who had been stuck in Dubai due to escalating conflict in the Middle East finally returned to Dar es Salaam on a special Air Tanzania flight. The passengers were visi...

item.title: Trump Administration Advances Drug Price Reduction Policies and Non-Coercive Vaccine Approaches
item.description: In this opinion article, physician Nicole Saphier praises President Donald Trump's efforts to address high prescription drug costs, which have long burdened American families. Key initiatives include ...

item.title: Robodebt inquiry clears Scott Morrison; two officials found corrupt
item.description: A National Anti-Corruption Commission inquiry into the controversial Robodebt scheme has cleared former prime minister Scott Morrison of corruption, while finding two other officials engaged in corrup...

item.title: Rafael Behr: How Trump’s reckless war on Iran undermines America’s credibility and global stability
item.description: Rafael Behr argues that Donald Trump’s military campaign against Iran, called Operation Epic Fury, exposes the incoherence and recklessness at the core of his foreign policy. Initially framed as a mis...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh CM Announces Plan for Indian School of Agriculture and Sets Governance Goals
item.description: Amaravati, Andhra Pradesh: Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu on Wednesday proposed the establishment of an 'Indian School of Agriculture' aimed at strengthening the agricultural sector, drawing inspi...

item.title: Tracking Maritime Activity and Incidents in the Strait of Hormuz Amid Closure
item.description: Iran's recent actions to restrict passage through the Strait of Hormuz have caused significant disruption to maritime traffic in the strategically vital waterway. While Iran cannot completely close th...

item.title: The Shifting Landscape of Global Health and Its Implications for South Africa
item.description: Recent funding cuts have pushed multilateral health institutions into crisis, raising serious implications for South Africa. Organisations like WHO and UNAIDS, once central to global health cooperatio...

item.title: Tanzania Olympic Committee schedules leadership elections on April 12 after constitutional amendments
item.description: The Tanzania Olympic Committee (TOC) is gearing up for its leadership elections set for April 12, 2026, in Dar es Salaam, after necessary constitutional amendments were approved. Initially, elections ...

item.title: Timmins Police Issue Numerous Charges During Two-Week Traffic Enforcement Campaign
item.description: Timmins Police recently completed a two-week traffic enforcement campaign, resulting in numerous charges being laid against drivers. The blitz focused on improving road safety and targeting common tra...

item.title: Several Countries Decline Tinubu’s Ambassador Nominations Over Short Time Left in Office
item.description: Some countries, including India, no gree accept Nigeria’s newly nominated ambassadors from President Bola Tinubu’s administration because dem dey worry say the government no get enough time left for o...

item.title: Omisore Sues APC Over Governorship Primary Disqualification in Osun
item.description: The wahala wey dey Osun State APC don enter another level as former National Secretary, Senator Iyiola Omisore, don drag the party go Federal High Court. This matter na because dem disqualify am and s...

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Injured but Secure Amid Conflict
item.description: Iran’s newly appointed Supreme Leader, Ayatullah Mojtaba Khamenei, has reportedly been injured but remains safe, according to the son of President Masoud Pezeshkian. Mojtaba, 56, rose to the top post ...

item.title: At least six dead after bus fire in Kerzers, Switzerland; authorities investigate deliberate act
item.description: At least six people have died and three others were injured after a bus caught fire in Kerzers, in the canton of Fribourg, western Switzerland. Police suspect the blaze may have been a deliberate act,...

item.title: Labour MP speaks out on delays in rape cases and justice reforms
item.description: Labour MP Charlotte Nichols revealed in the House of Commons that it took 1,088 days for her rape case to reach court, highlighting the prolonged delays faced by victims. She disclosed that she was ra...
